name: Cancel Alert
summary: Cancels a previously placed price alert. Send a POST request with a jData JSON object identifying the alert (al_id) plus the jKey session token; a successful response returns the alert id and a cancel/delete status indication.

request_params.name: jData*
request_params.description: Should send json object with fields in below list

json_fields.name: uid
json_fields.description: User id of the logged in user.

response_fields.name: request_time
response_fields.description: This will be present only in a successful response.

response_fields.name: emsg
response_fields.description: This will be present only in case of errors. That is : 1) Invalid Input 2) Session Expired

notes: The curl example uses a placeholder BaseURL; the real base is https://piconnect.flattrade.in/PiConnectAPI (full endpoint .../CancelAlert). Request is form-style: jData is a JSON object and jKey is appended as a separate body/query parameter (jData={...}&jKey=...). DOC INCONSISTENCY: the documented jData fields are uid (optional) and al_id* (mandatory), but the sample curl --data body instead contains "uid" and "actid" and does NOT include al_id — a copy-paste artifact from another endpoint; to cancel an alert you must supply al_id. request_time format is HH:MM:SS DD-MM-YYYY. In the PDF the al_id key/value renders with curly/smart quotes (“ ”) — a PDF artifact; real responses use standard JSON double quotes (transcribed here as straight quotes).
issues: The Cancel Alert success-response sample is split across the page boundary: page-60's right column shows the CancelAlert curl followed by a 'Sample Success Response :' block cut off at '[ {', which continues at the TOP of page-61's right column as request_time 15:03:33 / "Oi delete success" / al_id 21040800000008. The draft mis-bound the earlier '11:22:26 / Oi created / 21040800000004' block (that is the tail of the Place Alert response on page 60) to Cancel Alert; corrected here. The curl body's uid/actid fields are a doc error (do not match the documented al_id field), so the exact runtime accepted field name for the alert id cannot be confirmed from the curl alone — al_id* per the jData field table (page 61) is authoritative.
